title: Perinatal Substance Use Disorder Treatment - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Perinatal Substance Use Disorder Treatment 7 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details CODA GRESHAM RECOVERY CENTER WOMEN’S AND MEN’S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT WOMEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers gender-specific services for women, including those who are pregnant and parenting, and women involved with child welfare or criminal justice systems. Treatment supports women as they work on parenting in recovery and building and supporting safe and stable families and homes. Access to methadone services available to program participants. 1427 SE 182nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older; may include patients with cases in child welfare or criminal justice systems. Children may stay with their mothers on site. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 761-6005 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONTINUUM BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & RECOVERY SERVICES HOME PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides residential alcohol and drug treatment for up to 24 women with children. Services may include individual and group counseling, case management, life skills education, parenting education, health screening/monitoring, child care, and other ancillary programming. Eligibility: Pregnant or parenting women with children younger than age 5 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 772-4648 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWORKS NW LIFEWORKS NW BEAVERTON WOMEN'S RESIDENTIAL SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides residential substance use disorder services. 10700 SW Beaverton-Hillside Highway Suite 110 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Women with substance use disorder issues who are pregnant or have children under age 6 Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 645-9010 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OASIS CENTER OF THE ROGUE VALLEY PRENATAL AND POSTPARTUM CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Specializes in working with parents with substance use disorders. Provides prenatal and postpartum care, Medication-Assisted Treatment, family support services, and care coordination. 534 East Main Street Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Pregnant women and parents experiencing substance use disorders who have children under 6 years old Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-2pm Email (541) 200-1530 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON WASHINGTON HEALTH NETWORK NURTURE OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides substance use disorder treatment, which may include the following services: Education/rehabilitation, peer services, Medication Assisted Treatments, on-site drug and alcohol assessments, doula and maternity care for pregnant families. Participants can also access a weekly mother's support group. 200 SE Hailey Avenue Suite 307 Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Pregnant or postpartum individuals Hours: Support group: Monday 4pm-5pm All other services: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (541) 612-2255 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CHEMICAL DEPENDENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides evaluation, information and referral, prevention, and early intervention services for substance abuse disorder and/or alcohol use disorder. Offers outpatient and intensive outpatient treatment. 627 NE Evans Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-6:30pm (503) 434-7527 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CODA WOMEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT LIFEWORKS NW WOMEN'S RESIDENTIAL SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES OASIS CENTER OF THE ROGUE VALLEY PRENATAL AND POSTPARTUM CARE OREGON WASHINGTON HEALTH NETWORK NURTURE OREGON YAMHILL COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CHEMICAL DEPENDENCY SERVICES
